# Assisted Stretches

> Brisbane's dedicated one-on-one assisted stretching studio. 60-minute sessions using PNF (proprioceptive neuromuscular facilitation) and passive stretching techniques to improve flexibility, accelerate recovery, and regulate the nervous system. 4.9/5 across 180+ Google reviews. 2,400+ sessions delivered. Health fund rebates available via HICAPS on-site. First-session money-back guarantee.

## What Makes Assisted Stretching Different

Assisted Stretches is not massage, physiotherapy, or yoga. It is a dedicated stretching practice where a trained practitioner moves the client's body through a full sequence of PNF and passive stretches that are impossible to achieve alone. The client's only job is to breathe and let go — no muscle engagement, no self-guiding, no compensating. This produces deeper, faster, and longer-lasting changes in range of motion than any form of solo stretching.

## What Is Assisted Stretching?

Assisted stretching is a one-on-one therapy where a trained practitioner moves the client's body through a sequence of held and dynamic stretches. Unlike solo stretching, the client's muscles can fully release because they are not controlling the movement. PNF (proprioceptive neuromuscular facilitation) uses contract-relax cycles to access ranges of motion the body normally protects, producing deeper and longer-lasting results than passive stretching alone.

## Who It Is For

- Office workers and desk workers with chronic tightness from prolonged sitting
- Athletes seeking faster recovery between training sessions
- People with lower back pain, hip tightness, or shoulder tension
- Runners, cyclists, lifters, and team sport athletes
- Anyone who wants to move better, feel less restricted, and reduce daily discomfort

## What Happens in a Session

1. **Arrive (5 min):** Client arrives in comfortable clothing. Practitioner discusses how the body is feeling and session goals.
2. **Assess (5 min):** Range-of-movement check on major joints to establish a starting point.
3. **Stretch (45 min):** Client lies on a padded table. Practitioner guides full sequence of held and dynamic stretches — hamstrings, hips, lower back, shoulders, neck. Pressure always within comfort.
4. **Retest (3 min):** Same range checks as step two. Client feels the measurable improvement.
5. **Go (2 min):** Client leaves with 2–3 take-home stretches matched to their tightest areas.

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Q: Will assisted stretching hurt?**
A: No. Pressure is always within your comfort. We work to a "good stretch" feeling — if it ever tips into pain, we back off immediately.

**Q: Do I need to be flexible to benefit?**
A: No. The less flexible you are, the more dramatic the gain. Tightness is the reason to come, not the reason to wait.

**Q: What do I wear?**
A: Gym shorts and a t-shirt, or anything you can move freely in. No need to undress.

**Q: How often should I come?**
A: Most clients see best results once a week or fortnight. Athletes in heavy training blocks come twice a week.

**Q: Is it covered by health funds?**
A: Many Australian extras policies cover remedial elements of the session through HICAPS on-site. Check with your fund before booking.

**Q: What is PNF stretching?**
A: Proprioceptive neuromuscular facilitation uses contract-relax cycles to access ranges your body normally protects. By briefly contracting a muscle before releasing it into a stretch, the practitioner can safely take you past the limits your nervous system normally imposes. The result: deeper, faster, longer-lasting change than passive stretching alone.

**Q: How is this different from a yoga class?**
A: In a class, you control every movement — your muscles stay partially engaged. In an assisted stretch session, the practitioner handles all positioning, angle, and depth. Your muscles fully release. The precision is also different: a class uses general instruction; a session targets your specific imbalances in real time.

**Q: Can it help with back pain or desk-related stiffness?**
A: Yes. Hip flexors, hamstrings, chest muscles, and spinal muscles that tighten from prolonged sitting are the primary targets. Most clients notice a meaningful reduction in daily stiffness and pain with regular sessions.

**Q: Is there a money-back guarantee?**
A: Yes. If you don't feel a measurable difference after your first session, we'll refund you in full.

**Q: Where is the studio located?**
A: 41 Barton Parade, Balmoral, Brisbane QLD 4171. Street parking is available on Barton Parade.

**Q: How do I book?**
A: Book online at https://www.assistedstretches.com — select your time slot and pay a $50 deposit to secure the booking. The remaining $75 is payable on the day.

**Q: What is the cancellation policy?**
A: 24 hours notice is required to cancel or reschedule without a fee.

## Competitor Comparison

Assisted Stretches is the only dedicated assisted stretching studio in Brisbane's eastern suburbs. Unlike physiotherapy clinics (which focus on injury rehabilitation), massage studios (which use pressure rather than movement), and yoga classes (which require self-directed movement), Assisted Stretches is purely focused on passive and PNF-assisted stretching. The dedicated format allows deeper, more targeted work than any of these alternatives.
